perturbation.original_text: Schoolpop shall discontinue reselling Encompass(R) Select Cards on December 31, 2004 and shall effectively manage and forecast Encompass(R) Select Card needs to deplete such inventory as of December 31, 2004. In the event that any unsold Encompass(R) Select Cards remain in inventory by Schoolpop on December 31, 2004, Schoolpop shall provide to AEIS, no later than January 5, 2005, the Encompass(R) Select Card numbers, the respective expiration dates and denominations of the Cards. AEIS shall invoice Schoolpop for the applicable Card destruction fees which shall be payable by Schoolpop within thirty days (30) of receipt of invoice. Such Card list shall be submitted on a disk/CD and shall be sent via a form of shipment where signature is required upon delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: Schoolpop shall discontinue reselling Encompass(R) Select Cards on December 31, 2004 and shall effectively manage and forecast Encompass(R) Select Card needs to deplete such inventory as of December 31, 2004.  Schoolpop may continue to sell remaining Encompass(R) Select Cards without penalty for an additional 60 days.  In the event that any unsold Encompass(R) Select Cards remain in inventory by Schoolpop after February 29, 2005, Schoolpop shall provide to AEIS, no later than March 5, 2005, the Encompass(R) Select Card numbers, the respective expiration dates and denominations of the Cards. AEIS shall invoice Schoolpop for the applicable Card destruction fees which shall be payable by Schoolpop within thirty days (30) of receipt of invoice. Such Card list shall be submitted on a disk/CD and shall be sent via a form of shipment where signature is required upon delivery.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandates discontinuation of Encompass(R) Select Cards on December 31, 2004, with unsold cards subject to destruction fees. The modified version permits continued sales for an additional 60 days without penalty. This creates an in-text contradiction because in Section 9(a), it states 'Schoolpop shall discontinue reselling Encompass(R) Select Cards on December 31, 2004', directly conflicting with the allowance of extended sales in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the Effective Date through September 30, 2004, Schoolpop may order non-standard Encompass(R) Select denominations (other than 25, 50, 75, and 100 Points) up to a maximum of 200 Points. Such non-standard Encompass(R) Select denominations require eight (8) weeks to fulfill and must be ordered in boxes of 100 Cards of like denominations. Schoolpop shall be limited to $26,500,000 in total Encompass(R) Select sales through December 31, 2004. Such limit may be increased in the event Persona Select actual sales exceed its forecasted sales for the period ending December 31, 2004. The limit increase shall be calculated on three-to-one ratio of Encompass Select sales to Persona Select Sales. Schoolpop shall discontinue reselling Encompass(R) Select Cards on December 31, 2004 and shall effectively manage and forecast Encompass(R) Select Card needs to deplete such inventory as of December 31, 2004. In the event that any unsold Encompass Select Cards remain in inventory by Schoolpop on December 31, 2004, Schoolpop shall provide to AEIS, no later than January 5, 2005, the Encompass(R) Select Card numbers, the respective expiration dates and denominations of the Cards. Such Card list shall be provided to AEIS by Schoolpop via disk/CD and sent via a form of shipment where signature is required upon delivery. AEIS shall invoice Schoolpop for applicable destruction fees of $2.00 per Card which shall be payable by Schoolpop within thirty (30) days upon receipt of invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text in Section 9(a) states, "Schoolpop shall discontinue reselling Encompass(R) Select Cards on December 31, 2004," whereas the changed text allows Schoolpop to "continue to sell remaining Encompass(R) Select Cards without penalty for an additional 60 days." This creates an obvious conflict in the obligations of Schoolpop. The contradicted text mirrors this exact language, therefore creating a contradiction between the two texts.

perturbation.original_text: CARDS LOST OR STOLEN IN TRANSIT IN THE INITIAL SHIPMENT FROM AEIS WILL BE THE RESPONSIBILITY OF AEIS. The full risk of loss for ordered Cards shall pass to Schoolpop immediately upon their delivery to Schoolpop by AEIS and AEIS shall not reimburse Schoolpop or Client for any delivered Card that is lost, stolen, misappropriated, or that otherwise disappears or is fraudulently or inappropriately used (together referred to as "Loss"). Schoolpop shall remain solely liable for any Loss caused by the negligence or willful misconduct of its employees, agents or representatives after the shipment has been received by Schoolpop. AEIS will not reimburse Schoolpop or its Client or be responsible for any Loss in connection with or after delivery of Cards to Schoolpop, its Clients or the Cardholder whether they are shipped to Schoolpop, its Client, or to either of their pre-designated delivery agents. Only Cardholders are eligible to receive replacements from AEIS and replacements are given only in accordance with the agreement between the Cardholder and AEIS. Cards are usually shipped in packaging with AEIS' designated vendor as the return address. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AEIS shall bear no risk of Loss and of non-receipt of Cards by the intended Cardholder in the event that AEIS is instructed to use another address as the return address, the address provided to AEIS for Schoolpop or the Client was incorrect, the request for the Card was unauthorized, and/or any information received by AEIS with regard to the processing of the Order was fraudulent or incorrect.
perturbation.changed_text: CARDS LOST OR STOLEN IN TRANSIT IN THE INITIAL SHIPMENT FROM AEIS WILL BE THE RESPONSIBILITY OF AEIS unless Schoolpop has failed to follow AEIS's shipping instructions. The full risk of loss for ordered Cards shall pass to Schoolpop immediately upon their delivery to Schoolpop by AEIS and AEIS shall not reimburse Schoolpop or Client for any delivered Card that is lost, stolen, misappropriated, or that otherwise disappears or is fraudulently or inappropriately used (together referred to as "Loss"). Schoolpop shall remain solely liable for any Loss caused by the negligence or willful misconduct of its employees, agents or representatives after the shipment has been received by Schoolpop. AEIS will not reimburse Schoolpop or its Client or be responsible for any Loss in connection with or after delivery of Cards to Schoolpop, its Clients or the Cardholder whether they are shipped to Schoolpop, its Client, or to either of their pre-designated delivery agents. Only Cardholders are eligible to receive replacements from AEIS and replacements are given only in accordance with the agreement between the Cardholder and AEIS. Cards are usually shipped in packaging with AEIS' designated vendor as the return address. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AEIS shall bear no risk of Loss and of non-receipt of Cards by the intended Cardholder in the event that AEIS is instructed to use another address as the return address, the address provided to AEIS for Schoolpop or the Client was incorrect, the request for the Card was unauthorized, and/or any information received by AEIS with regard to the processing of the Order was fraudulent or incorrect.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states AEIS is responsible for cards lost or stolen during the initial shipment. The modified version introduces an exception if Schoolpop fails to follow AEIS's shipping instructions. However, the agreement does not explicitly define 'shipping instructions.' If the original text specifies that 'CARDS LOST OR STOLEN IN TRANSIT IN THE INITIAL SHIPMENT FROM AEIS WILL BE THE RESPONSIBILITY OF AEIS' however, now the text dictates that AEIS won't be responsible for the first initial lost.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CARDS LOST OR STOLEN IN TRANSIT IN THE INITIAL SHIPMENT FROM AEIS WILL BE THE RESPONSIBILITY OF AEIS unless Schoolpop has failed to follow AEIS's shipping instructions. The full risk of loss for ordered Cards shall pass to Schoolpop immediately upon their delivery to Schoolpop by AEIS and AEIS shall not reimburse Schoolpop or Client for any delivered Card that is lost, stolen, misappropriated, or that otherwise disappears or is fraudulently or inappropriately used (together referred to as "Loss"). Schoolpop shall remain solely liable for any Loss caused by the negligence or willful misconduct of its employees, agents or representatives after the shipment has been received by Schoolpop. AEIS will not reimburse Schoolpop or its Client or be responsible for any Loss in connection with or after delivery of Cards to Schoolpop, its Clients or the Cardholder whether they are shipped to Schoolpop, its Client, or to either of their pre-designated delivery agents. Only Cardholders are eligible to receive replacements from AEIS and replacements are given only in accordance with the agreement between the Cardholder and AEIS. Cards are usually shipped in packaging with AEIS' designated vendor as the return address. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AEIS shall bear no risk of Loss and of non-receipt of Cards by the intended Cardholder in the event that AEIS is instructed to use another address as the return address, the address provided to AEIS for Schoolpop or the Client was incorrect, the request for the Card was unauthorized, and/or any information received by AEIS with regard to the processing of the Order was fraudulent or incorrect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'CARDS LOST OR STOLEN IN TRANSIT IN THE INITIAL SHIPMENT FROM AEIS WILL BE THE RESPONSIBILITY OF AEIS'. The changed text introduces an exception: 'CARDS LOST OR STOLEN IN TRANSIT IN THE INITIAL SHIPMENT FROM AEIS WILL BE THE RESPONSIBILITY OF AEIS unless Schoolpop has failed to follow AEIS's shipping instructions.' This creates an ambiguity and potential contradiction. The term 'shipping instructions' is undefined, making it unclear when AEIS is responsible. This ambiguity directly contradicts the original statement of AEIS's responsibility, creating an in-text contradiction according to definition 1 of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: AEIS may from time to time provide Schoolpop with certain reasonable terms and provisions to be included in the agreements to be entered into between Schoolpop and its Clients and Schoolpop agrees that any such terms and provisions shall be included in such agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: AEIS may from time to time provide Schoolpop with certain reasonable terms and provisions to be included in the agreements to be entered into between Schoolpop and its Clients and Schoolpop agrees that any such terms and provisions may, at Schoolpop's discretion, be included in such agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that Schoolpop 'agrees' to include terms provided by AEIS in its client agreements. The revised version changes this to 'may, at Schoolpop's discretion, be included,' creating a contradiction since it's no longer a mandatory inclusion. The original text specify that AEIS decides on certain provisions, while the new text specify the option for Schoolpop to decide
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schoolpop agrees that it will indemnify and hold AEIS harmless for any claims or losses resulting from its failure to comply with the requirements of Sections 4 (k), (m-n), unless due to the acts or omissions of AEIS.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Schoolpop to include AEIS's terms in client agreements, while the changed text grants Schoolpop discretion over their inclusion. This directly contradicts the obligation imposed in the original text. The contradicted text requires Schoolpop to indemnify AEIS for failures related to certain sections, which could arise from not including AEIS's terms as originally mandated. The change creates ambiguity regarding whether Schoolpop is still obligated to include those terms, thus potentially impacting the enforceability of the indemnification clause. Therefore, according to 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' from the definitions, this is a strong in-text contradiction.
